title: aquilegia vulgaris var. sulphurea
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. sulphurea, also known as the yellow columbine or sulphur columbine, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which can be beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris var. sulphurea include its ability to soothe irritated mucous membranes, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, and it is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine.
